questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following statements best describes the nature of the duties and operational scope of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India?

options.text: The CAG exercises control over the issue of money from the Consolidated Fund of India before expenditure is incurred.
The CAG's reports on the execution of projects are primarily discussed by the Estimates Committee of Parliament.
Information from CAG reports can be utilized by investigating agencies to press charges in a court of law.
The CAG is a statutory body whose primary mandate is defined by the Finance Act of 1971.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct: Information from CAG reports can be used by investigating agencies. Option A is incorrect: The CAG's duty arises only after expenditure has been incurred (ex-post facto). Option B is incorrect: CAG reports are discussed by the Public Accounts Committee (PAC), not the Estimates Committee. Option D is incorrect: The CAG is a Constitutional Body (Article 148), though its duties are detailed in the CAG Act of 1971.
questions.takeaway: The CAG acts as an ex-post facto auditor whose findings have legal utility for investigating agencies and are scrutinized by the Public Accounts Committee.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Duty of the CAG arises only after the expenditure has been incurred. CAG reports on the execution of projects or programs by the ministers are discussed by the Public Accounts Committee. Information from CAG reports can be used by investigating agencies to press charges in court of law.
